data.title: Marmota Extends South Australia Titanium Discovery
data.description:
Marmota said the potential strike of its titanium heavy minerals sands discovery at Muckanippie in South Australia was extended after it identified leucoxene in historical works by Flinders Mines, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

It said 100% leucoxene was observed in all heavy mineral concentrate samples in historical work at its tenement, west of the discovery at Muckanippie.

Leucoxene is a high-value, high-titanium-content mineral.

Its shares rose 4% on market close on Monday.

data.title: Redeye: Sivers Semiconductors - Don’t miss the forest for the trees
data.description:
Redeye notes that Sivers’ Q4 2024 report was strong, with record net sales of SEK76.7m driven by accelerating SATCOM deployments and new contracts. Management indicates a new baseline for revenue going into 2025, setting up the stage for a transformative 2025 when SATCOM volumes are expected to ramp by the end of the year. We keep our Base Case of SEK6.2 intact and provide additional comments about our view of the company, its future prospects, and how our long-term estimates are derived.

data.title: Fraser and Neave Strikes Off Dormant Malaysian Subsidiary
data.description:
Fraser and Neave struck off its direct dormant subsidiary, Tiger Tavern, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Friday.

The striking off of the Malaysian subsidiary is not expected to impact the group's earnings per share for the fiscal year ending Sept. 30.

data.title: Sasol 1H EBIT ZAR9.53B
Copper Prices Stabilize as Traders Assess Market Outlook
data.description:
Copper futures held steady at approximately $4.55 per pound on Monday, recovering from last week’s weak performance.

Prices were supported by ongoing concerns about a tightening supply.

Traders are also closely monitoring the potential impact of escalating tariff threats from US President Donald Trump, although copper has so far been excluded from his remarks.

On Friday, officials from Washington and Beijing held a phone call, sparking optimism that a trade deal between the two nations may be within reach.

In the meantime, China, the world’s largest copper consumer, has unveiled a 2025 action plan to boost foreign investment, addressing economic challenges amid rising geopolitical tensions.

data.title: Ades Holding Posts Higher FY24 Net Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Ades Holding Co. (SASE:2382) said Monday its full-year 2024 profit and revenue rose year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 816.2 million Saudi riyals, compared with 452.1 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 0.73 riyal from 0.59 riyal.

The Saudi Arabia-listed drilling services company's revenue was 6.20 billion riyals, compared with 4.33 billion riyals a year ago.

Ades' board plans to pay a dividend of 0.22 riyal per share for the second half of 2024. The dividend will be paid March 24, 2025, with an eligibility date of March 10, 2025. It paid 0.216 riyal per share for first half of 2024.

data.title: Amer New Material Changes Name
data.description:
Jiangsu Amer New Material officially changed its name to Jiangsu Jiuding New Material after completing registration procedures, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Friday.

The Chinese fiber-reinforced plastic manufacturer's English abbreviation was amended to Jiuding from Amer New Material, but its stock code remains, the disclosure said.

The company's shares rose more than 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Palm follows rival Dalian and Chicago oils lower
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures fell more than 1% on Monday, weighed down by weakness in rival Dalian and Chicago oils, while weak demand from top buyer India also pressured the market.

The benchmark palm oil contract for May delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange lost 73 ringgit, or 1.57%, to 4,591 ringgit ($1,044.36) a metric ton by the midday break.

Sharp losses in rival oils dampened market sentiment, said a Kuala Lumpur-based trader.

Dalian's most-active soyoil contract (DBYcv1) was down 0.38%, while its palm oil contract fell 2%. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade (BOc2) were down 0.7%.

Palm oil tracks price movements of rival edible oils, as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

India's palm oil imports in the 2024/25 marketing year ending in October could fall to as low as 7.5 million tons, the lowest in five years, the head of an industry body said, as its rising premium over soyoil and sunflower oil pushes refiners towards more affordable alternatives.

Indian refiners have cancelled orders for 100,000 tons of crude palm oil scheduled for delivery between March and June because of a surge in benchmark Malaysian prices and negative refining margins in India, four trade sources said.

India is likely to raise import taxes on vegetable oils for the second time in less than six months to help support thousands of oilseed farmers reeling from a crash in domestic oilseed prices, two government sources said.

Malaysian palm oil exports to China will "remain resilient" this year despite their premium over rival oils and Chinese consumers' shifting buying patterns, deputy commodities minister Chan Foong Hin said.

Palm oil may fall to 4,583 ringgit per ton, following its failure to break resistance and a drop below a rising channel.

($1 = 4.3960 ringgit)

data.title: Nuode New Materials' Subsidiary Signs 2025 Supply Agreement
data.description:
Nuode New Materials said its wholly-owned subsidiary, Shenzhen Baijiada New Energy Materials, signed a 2025 supply framework agreement with CALB Group, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai Bourse.

Baijiada will supply 45,000 tons of copper foil in 2025, with the actual supply based on sales orders. The deal will positively impact Nord's 2025 performance. The contract remains valid until the 2026 supply framework agreement takes effect.

ECB's Wunsch warns of risk of 'sleepwalking' into excessive rate cuts, FT reports
data.description:
European Central Bank policymaker Pierre Wunsch said the euro zone faces the risk of "sleepwalking" into excessive interest rate cuts and must be prepared to stop soon, the Financial Times reported on Monday.

The Belgian central bank governor said he felt "relatively comfortable" with the market expectation of 2% rates by the end of the year "give or take 50 basis points," the FT said.

On Jan. 30, the ECB cut its deposit rate by 25 basis points to 2.75%, and policymakers guided for a further reduction in March as concerns over lacklustre economic growth supersede worries about persistent inflation.

"I'm not pleading for a pause in April but we must not sleepwalk to 2% (interest rates) without thinking about it. If the data justify a new cut, we'll cut. If they don't, we might have to pause," the Financial Times quoted Wunsch as saying on Friday.

He also highlighted growing uncertainty over the appropriate level of interest rates, saying he was "not even sure" whether rates remained restrictive for growth and inflation.

The ECB has cut interest rates five times since last June and policymakers are increasingly vocal in their debate about just how much farther rates must come down when inflation is still a bit too high but economic growth is barely above zero.

data.title: Emperor Culture Posts Lower H1 Loss; Shares Slide 13%
data.description:
Emperor Culture Group reported a loss attributable to the owners of HK$56.8 million for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, compared with HK$143.2 million in the previous year, according to a Friday filing with the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

Loss per share was HK$0.018, compared with HK$0.045 in the prior-year period.

The company's revenue fell to HK$243.1 million from HK$267.2 million the previous year, which Emperor Culture attributed to weak consumption and the lack of blockbusters.

The company's shares were down over 13% in recent trading.

data.title: Sasol skips dividend after profit declines 31%
data.description:
South African petrochemical firm Sasol on Monday skipped paying a dividend after reporting a 31% decline in half-year profit on a fall in oil prices and lower sales volumes.

Sasol's headline earnings per share for the six months to December 2024 were 14.13 rand ($0.7712), down from 20.37 rand during the same period a year earlier.

The company, which produces fuel and chemicals from coal and gas, said its revenue was 10% lower at 122.1 billion rand, mainly due to a 13% drop in the average rand price per barrel of Brent crude oil and a significant decline in refining margins.

Sasol also reported a 5% decrease in sales volumes as a result of lower production and lower market demand.

The company said it did not declare a dividend because it ended the period with a negative free cash flow of 1.1 billion rand, while its net debt of $4.3 billion exceeded levels set in its capital allocation policy.

($1 = 18.3223 rand)

data.title: FX option expiries for 24 February 10am New York cut
data.description:
There are a couple to take note of on the day, as highlighted in bold.

They are for EUR/USD at the 1.0500 and 1.0525 levels. The euro is underpinned after the German election results over the weekend. The result sees Friedrich Merz's CDU/CSU alliance take charge, with coalition negotiations now in the works. They'll have to either team up with SPD and Greens as potential partners. Given how the SPD's influence is waning, Merz is likely to want to team up with the Greens instead surely.

The CDU/CSU alliance have been largely in favour of maintaining Germany's debt brake but if they are to rely on support from the Greens, perhaps they may have to work through some reforms on that.

If Germany moves to relax the debt brake - allowing for more public investment into infrastructure, green energy, etc - then that could bolster growth and lead to increased government spending in the short-term.

Those are factors in favour of a stronger euro and a better outlook for euro area assets. But if it threatens to destabilise debt concerns, then that might have an adverse impact on the euro in the long-term. That being said, Germany has one of the lowest debt-to-GDP ratios in the region, so there's no need to worry too much for now.

Circling back to the expiries, with the euro underpinned as such, we could be eyeing at a firm break of 1.0500. That will see the expiries act as a defensive layer in keeping the single currency more bid throughout the day as buyers look to use that as a platform for a stronger upside break this week.

For more information on how to use this data, you may refer to this post here.

data.title: Nadec Logs Higher FY24 Net Profit, Revenue
data.description:
The National Agricultural Development (SASE:6010), d/b/a Nadec, said Monday its net profit and revenue for the full year 2024 increased year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 774.6 million Saudi riyals, compared with 302.1 million riyals a year ago. EPS moved to 2.57 riyals from 1.77 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-listed agricultural company's revenue was 3.22 billion riyals, compared with 3.2 billion riyals earlier.

data.title: Redeye: Dignitana Q4’24 - Improving sales momentum
data.description:
Redeyes’ long-term view of Dignitana continues to be positive. The sales improved more than expected, and the company likely has a new momentum. The cost control was reasonable. The market fundamentals are still positive, with the CPT 1 codes and CMS decision. The market likes the indication that a rights issue is likely out of the question. We have decreased our expectations slightly while the new year, and fine-tuning the balance sheet is a positive factor. With these minor changes, we lower our Base case value to SEK 5.3 (5.5) per share, with a fair value range of SEK 1.6 (1.8) to 14 (13) per share. Following this report, we also change the lead analyst.
